summary: Publish runtime constraints and safe constraint decision projection in the production contract.
rationale: This slice adds an explicit runtime constraints contract, centralizes public stop-reason normalization in a shared runtime policy, and projects existing runtime-only constraints into candidate-level public decisions without changing orchestrator flow or provider behavior.
verification: uv run pytest tests/test_runtime_constraints.py tests/test_runtime_production_contract.py tests/test_api_production_contract.py tests/test_runtime_source_degradation.py tests/test_runtime_core_commit.py -q

uv run ruff check src/seektalent/api.py src/seektalent/runtime/constraints.py src/seektalent/runtime/production_contract.py src/seektalent/runtime/stop_reasons.py src/seektalent/runtime/orchestrator.py tests/test_runtime_constraints.py tests/test_api_production_contract.py
uv run ty check src tests tools
uv run pytest tests/test_liepin_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_opencli_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_cli.py tests/test_liepin_boundaries.py -q
deletion_targets: production stop reasons that leak raw controller or provider text
runtime-only constraints that are invisible in the production candidate contract
hard runtime limits that are implicit in settings but absent from a public runtime constraints contract
risks: This slice intentionally projects only existing runtime-only constraints; provider verification behavior remains owned by later runtime/provider slices.
The stop reason allowlist is centralized in a shared runtime policy and consumed by both the orchestrator and production projection.
